sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
Per-gene, exon-level, target-region, platform, read-length, and quality-filter details are not supplied.
Benchmark truth-set comparison, assay validation, and clinical sensitivity evidence are not supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived summary reports mean autosomal depth of 31.4x and median autosomal depth of 30.8x after upstream filtering.
resultRows.sourceLabel: SAMtools depth; GATK/Picard CollectWgsMetrics; GATK WgsMetrics
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This metric describes the typical amount of sequencing evidence in the supplied summary. It does not identify variants and does not diagnose or rule out any condition.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived summary reports 98.7% of assessed autosomal positions at or above 10x coverage, 95.2% at or above 20x coverage, and 0.8% with zero observed coverage.
resultRows.sourceLabel: SAMtools coverage; GATK/Picard CollectWgsMetrics; GATK WgsMetrics
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Breadth describes how much of the assessed genome reaches selected depth thresholds. It cannot say whether a specific gene, exon, or variant was adequately assessed unless regional data is supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived summary reports a coverage coefficient of variation of 0.18 and 88.6% of assessed autosomal bases between 0.5x and 1.5x the mean depth.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Uniformity helps explain whether coverage is evenly distributed. This package cannot name high- or low-coverage intervals because those interval details are not present.

resultRows.item: Regional, platform, benchmark, and clinical validation context
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Per-gene and exon-level region tables, capture target definition, platform chemistry, read length, quality-filter thresholds, ploidy handling, benchmark truth-set comparison, and authenticated sample-report rows are unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NIST Genome in a Bottle; GATK/Picard CollectWgsMetrics
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can summarize supplied QC metrics, but it cannot validate assay performance, clinical sensitivity, or variant detection from these inputs alone.

coverageContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Sequencing depth and coverage are quality-control metrics. They describe how much sequencing evidence is available across assessed positions, not what variants are present or what those variants mean medically.
unsupportedClaims: clinical sensitivity or negative predictive value
screening, diagnostic testing, reproductive, lifestyle, or treatment recommendation
coverageContext.professionalReview: Laboratory or clinical review is needed to interpret coverage against a specific assay, target region list, validation study, and reporting threshold.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: mean autosomal depth 31.4x; median autosomal depth 30.8x
98.7% at or above 10x; 95.2% at or above 20x; 0.8% zero observed coverage
coefficient of variation 0.18; 88.6% between 0.5x and 1.5x the mean depth
regional, platform, quality-filter, benchmark, and clinical validation context unavailable

missingInputs: Per-gene, exon-level, and interval-level coverage table
Capture target definition or callable region mask
Platform chemistry, read length, duplicate rate, and quality-filter thresholds
Assay validation and clinical sensitivity evidence
uncertainty: No calibrated model is supplied for variant detection probability, clinical sensitivity, or overall sample quality probability.
The selected metrics are useful QC context but cannot validate a sequencing assay or clinical report by themselves.
limitations: This validation result uses selected derived QC metrics only; raw genome data and raw sequencing/alignment files such as FASTQ, BAM, and CRAM are not included.
Coverage metrics are not variant calls and do not prove that any specific variant is present or absent.

The package does not diagnose disease, estimate medical risk, validate clinical sensitivity, or recommend medication, screening, diagnostic testing, reproductive decisions, lifestyle changes, or treatment changes.
